summary: Reigning world champions and the model's clear favourite (champ ~25% vs a ~9% market), FIFA 3rd, topping CONMEBOL qualifying nine points clear. A tournament-hardened core under Lionel Scaloni — World Cup 2022 sandwiched by back-to-back Copa Américas — built around a robust spine (E. Martínez, Otamendi, De Paul, J. Álvarez) and, when fit, Messi.
style: Nominal 4-3-3 with fluidity going forward — full-backs Tagliafico and Molina stretch the pitch and push high, enabling central rotations (midfielders dropping to build, attackers coming narrow for quick combinations). Wide overloads let Enzo Fernández and Mac Allister crash the box onto crosses and cutbacks, with late Messi runs and the last-line threat of Álvarez/Lautaro.

counterReliance.note: Controls through midfield but lethal vertically; mid-block out of possession

strengths: Elite tournament experience and a winning spine together since 2021
Midfield control (De Paul, Mac Allister, Fernández) plus full-back width for cutback chances
Match-winners from all angles — Messi's creation, box-crashing 8s, Álvarez/Lautaro on the last line
Emiliano Martínez's shootout edge in the knockouts
weaknesses: Ageing in key areas — fitness across a long run is a question; full-back depth thinner
Tend to start slowly (1-1 v Iceland 2018, 1-2 loss to Saudi Arabia 2022) before finishing strongly

keyPlayers.name: Lionel Messi
keyPlayers.note: The single biggest swing factor — across the 2021-22 winning runs, 21% of attacking-third touches and 28% of chances created. Most World Cup games ever; four goals from passing Klose's all-time record; a record sixth tournament

keyPlayers.name: Julián Álvarez
keyPlayers.note: Heir to the focal role — intelligent off-ball decoy runs (support runs/dropping off) that open space for the rotating attack

tactics: Let lesser sides have the ball and strike vertically, or patiently break a low block via Messi in pockets and full-back overloads into cutbacks. The plan rarely changes; the personnel adapt to the opponent.
mismatch: Vulnerable to high-energy pressing sides that deny the midfield time; dominant against teams that play open, end-to-end football.
xFactor: Messi's fitness and minutes management across a 39-day tournament in tough conditions.
notes: 21-year-old Como creator Nico Paz is the exciting young wildcard in the squad.
History suggests an early group slip needn't derail them — they've recovered from slow starts to go deep before.
sources: The Athletic — 2026 World Cup tactical guide, Group J (5 Jun 2026)
